analysis.period_summary: The funnel generated $1,138.83 from 36 front-end sales (Mar 9–14). Email traffic delivered 17 conversions at $0.41 RPV, while paid Facebook spend of $358.72 returned only 4 purchases at $89.68 CPA—above the profitable threshold. The biggest opportunity is scaling campaign #88 (V2 variant) which shows healthy 4.29% LP CTR and $50 CPA.

northstar.commentary: Email RPV of $0.41 is above the $0.28–0.35 benchmark, but paid RPV at $0.85 is below the $1.00 profitability threshold despite total funnel value averaging $31.63.

needle_movers.why: Campaign #88 Facebook delivered 1 purchase at $43.90 CPA with 4.6% LP CTR—well within healthy 4–6% range and profitable at $50.09 total spend for $62.33 revenue. This is the only paid campaign showing both strong engagement and profitability this week.
what: Double daily budget on #88 Facebook to $100/day and monitor CPA over 7 days
Pause or reduce #78 'InteractiveVSL CopyV11' ($137 spend, 0 conversions) and reallocate to #88
Test 2–3 new ad creatives within #88 structure to expand reach while maintaining LP CTR above 4%
needle_movers.revenue_impact: At 4.6% LP CTR, 0.29% CR, and $31.63 avg value, scaling to 500 impressions/day = 23 LP views/day × 7 days = 161 views/week. At 0.29% CR = 0.5 sales/week but at scale could hit 2–3 sales = $63–95/week gross margin after $700 spend.

needle_movers.area: Fix OTO2 Divine Helper take rate (5.6%)
needle_movers.why: OTO2 converted only 2 of 36 buyers (5.6%) for $44.16 revenue. Healthy OTO take rates should be 40%+, and this is the lowest-performing upsell in the funnel. OTO1 achieved 25% main + 8.3% downsell = 33.3% combined, showing buyers are receptive to upsells but OTO2 messaging or offer is failing.
what: A/B test OTO2 sales page headline and first 30 seconds of copy—focus on immediate transformation vs. OTO1's longer-term benefit
Add scarcity element (24-hour discount or bonus) to OTO2 page
Review OTO2 downsell redirect—if $77 downsell exists but shows $0 revenue for 10+ days, it's likely a broken link
needle_movers.revenue_impact: At 40% OTO2 take rate on 36 buyers = 14 sales × $97 = $1,358 vs. current $44. Net gain = $1,314/week or $5,256/month. Even hitting 20% = additional $600/week.

needle_movers.why: Three email campaigns (#54 with 42 views, #80 with 26 views, #81 with 20 views) have generated 0 conversions and $0 revenue. Combined 88 views at $0.41 expected email RPV = $36 in lost opportunity cost that could be redirected to working campaigns.
what: Immediately pause #54 (Maropost Headlines Split), #80 (Reactivation), #81 (Education) in CPV Labs
Redirect those email list segments to #87 (V2 Interactive VSL) which is converting at 0.55% and $0.30 RPV
If campaigns were testing new messaging, document learnings and archive—do not re-enable without new creative
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Reallocating 88 views/week to #87 at 0.55% CR and $31.63 avg value = 0.48 additional sales = $15/week immediate gain. Over 12 weeks = $180 recovered revenue.

needle_movers.why: GA4 shows /destiny (V1 full-screen) loses 88.6% of visitors before they interact with the choice buttons at ~4min mark (2,301 sessions → 262 choice_selected). This is the single largest drop-off point in the funnel. V2 also struggles here (5.99% choice rate) but has lower traffic volume.
what: Add a 'scroll to continue' or 'tap to reveal' interaction at 2min mark to re-engage users before the 4min choice point
Test showing choice buttons earlier (at 3min instead of 4min) for 50% of V1 traffic
Add exit-intent popup on V1 landing page with '1-question quiz' fallback to capture emails from bounces
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Improving V1 choice rate from 11.39% to 15% on 2,301 sessions = 83 additional choice events. At 56.87% choice-to-optin1 rate and 6.43% overall checkout rate = 5 additional checkouts/week × $31.63 = $158/week or $632/month.

needle_movers.why: CPV Labs shows $1,295.48 revenue vs. ClickBank $1,138.83—a $156.65 (13.76%) discrepancy. While CPV conversions (36) match ClickBank sales exactly, the revenue mismatch suggests either duplicate postback firing, incorrect SKU pricing in CPV, or refunds not tracked in CPV.
what: Check CPV Labs postback logs for duplicate transaction IDs on same timestamp
Verify CPV campaign payout settings match current ClickBank product prices (Basic $37, Advanced $54, OTO1 $67, etc.)
Cross-reference ClickBank 'Refunds' report for Mar 9–14 to see if $156 represents post-sale refunds not yet reflected in CPV
needle_movers.revenue_impact: No direct revenue impact but affects campaign profitability calculations. If $156 is phantom revenue in CPV, paid campaigns may appear more profitable than reality—critical for scale decisions.

scorecard.status_reason: 4.6% LP CTR, 1 sale at $43.90 CPA, profitable
Instagram: 1 sale at $12.85 CPA, but only 4 LP views
2 sales at $49.31 CPA, but 1.96% LP CTR below benchmark
5.6% take rate far below 40% benchmark, only 2 sales
$0 revenue, no data in SKU breakdown, likely broken redirect

analysis.data_notes: CPV Labs reports $1,295.48 revenue vs. ClickBank $1,138.83—a $156.65 (13.76%) gap despite matching conversion counts (36). Investigate duplicate postbacks, SKU pricing mismatches, or untracked refunds. OTO2 Downsell ($77) appears in funnel structure but has no sales data in SKU breakdown for full 6-day period—likely broken redirect. Email campaigns #51 and #88 show Facebook as source but are paid campaigns tracked in both CPV and paid_campaigns object—verify attribution is not double-counting.
